title: Secrets and key rotation playbook
purpose: Operational process for secure key and secret rotation during the hybrid Firebase/Supabase period.

rotationPolicy.emergencyRotation: Immediate rotation on suspected compromise or unauthorized access signal.
policyRules: No plaintext secrets in source control.
All secrets must have owner, creation timestamp, expiry timestamp, and last rotation timestamp.
Each rotation requires documented verification and rollback path.

rolesAndResponsibilities.securityOwner: Approves policy exceptions and emergency rotation execution.
rolesAndResponsibilities.platformOwner: Executes server-side secret updates and validates service health.
rolesAndResponsibilities.clientOwner: Coordinates client key changes and staged rollout where applicable.
rolesAndResponsibilities.releaseOwner: Schedules maintenance windows and signs off rotation completion.
rolesAndResponsibilities.onCall: Monitors alerts during and after rotation; executes rollback if required.

standardRotationWorkflow.name: Prepare
actions: Confirm inventory entry and dependency map.
Create change record and maintenance window if production-impacting.
Generate new credential/key in approved secret manager.

standardRotationWorkflow.name: Dual publish
actions: Store new secret alongside old secret (versioned).
Update consumers to accept new version while old remains valid.
Enable canary validation against health checks and smoke tests.

standardRotationWorkflow.name: Cutover
actions: Validate auth, request, note/history, media, and migration pathways.

standardRotationWorkflow.name: Revoke old secret
actions: Disable/revoke prior version after stabilization window.
Confirm no residual usage in logs/telemetry.

firebase: Rotate Firebase service-account keys with least-privilege scope and short overlap.
Revalidate callable function auth and storage access after rotation.
Disable legacy Firebase keys once platform path no longer depends on them.
supabase: Rotate service role keys and JWT signing secrets using staged environment rollout.
Validate RLS policy behavior and API access tokens post-rotation.
Ensure migration workers and platform-api both consume updated credentials.
crossSystemOrder: For production, rotate backend/service credentials before client-distributed keys when feasible.
Never rotate mutually dependent credentials simultaneously without rollback checkpoint.

triggers: Auth failures exceed threshold after cutover
Client login/request failures linked to rotated credential
steps: Revert consumer configuration to previous secret version.
Restart impacted services and validate core health probes.
Keep new key disabled until root cause remediation is verified.
Record incident timeline and remediation actions.

validationChecklist: Secret version updated in all intended environments
No secret value leakage in logs or build output
Core workflows pass smoke tests post-rotation
Old key usage drops to zero before revocation
Audit log records actor, time, and change ticket link

monitoringAndAlerting.alertPolicy: Page on-call for SEV1 thresholds; notify security owner for suspected compromise.
